requirements.text: `.planning/current-activity.json` is written atomically at every major workflow state transition (execute-phase, plan-phase, debug, quorum, circuit-breaker, new-milestone stages)

Activity schema: `{ activity, sub_activity, phase?, plan?, wave?, debug_round?, checkpoint?, quorum_round?, updated }` — all fields except `activity` and `updated` are optional; unknown fields are preserved
`gsd-tools.cjs activity-set <json>` CLI command writes the current-activity.json file with atomic replace; `activity-clear` removes it
`resume-work` reads current-activity.json and routes to the exact recovery point — displaying the interrupted state before resuming execution
`execute-phase` writes activity at every stage boundary: entering/exiting plan execution, checkpoint:verify, debug loop rounds, awaiting_human_verify, and verifying_phase
`plan-phase`, `new-milestone`, `debug`, `quorum`, and `circuit-breaker` resolution workflows write activity at every stage boundary
Activity file is cleared on successful completion of any top-level workflow; it persists across context resets when mid-workflow
Config values are normalized through a bidirectional adapter (e.g., profile names, boolean strings, nested vs flat keys) so internal types never leak to config files and vice versa
Quorum debates in `.planning/quorum/debates/` follow a consistent template (Context, Question, Positions, Decision, Consequences) that downstream tools can parse
User can add a new claude-mcp-server instance (name, provider, model, key)
User can remove an existing agent from the roster
Wizard runs identity ping to verify connectivity after provisioning new agent
All 11 TLA+ model files contain `@requirement` structured comments on each property/invariant, mapping it to specific requirement IDs
All 8 Alloy model files contain `@requirement` structured comments on each assertion/check, mapping it to specific requirement IDs
All 3 PRISM .props files contain `@requirement` structured comments on each property, mapping it to specific requirement IDs
`bin/extract-annotations.cjs` parses `@requirement` comments from TLA+, Alloy, and PRISM files and returns a structured JSON map of `{ model_file: [{ property, requirement_ids }] }`
The traceability matrix generator reads extracted annotations as a primary data source, with model-registry `requirements` arrays as fallback
All formal models declared in `.formal/model-registry.json` as single source of truth with provenance tracking
`promote-model.cjs` provides atomic promotion from per-phase specs to canonical specs with duplicate detection
`accept-debug-invariant.cjs` writes debug-discovered invariants directly to canonical specs with session provenance
QGSD, as a Claude Code plugin, MUST NOT bundle LLM SDKs; Haiku/Sonnet/Opus calls MUST use the Agent tool's model parameter
`hooks/dist/` rebuilt from current source — includes all circuit breaker hook code from Phases 6–8 and GUARD 5 code from Phase 5
`npm test` passes with 0 failures across all test suites (config-loader, stop hook, circuit breaker)

When circuit breaker fires, the oscillation trigger pattern (file set, alternation count, time window) is persisted to `.planning/formal/evidence/` so future sessions can preemptively avoid the same signature
`.formal/policy.yaml` is the single authoritative source for PRISM calibration parameters
`run-prism.cjs` reads `policy.yaml` and writes `writeCheckResult` with `observation_window` metadata
`read-policy.cjs` exposes all policy fields with typed interface
`policy.conservative_priors.tp_rate` and `policy.conservative_priors.unavail` from `.formal/policy.yaml` wire directly to `run-prism.cjs` fallback constants (v0.19-10 fix)
Test suite runs on every commit
Lint and type checks run in CI
Build artifacts are reproducible
Automated test suite runs on every pull request and merge to main is blocked when tests fail
Linting and formatting checks run in CI and block merge on violations
Type checking (if applicable to the language) runs in CI and blocks merge on type errors
CI/CD workflows trigger and test on both main and staging branches, run tests across Node 18/20/22 matrix, verify npm package integrity, and auto-publish @staging dist-tag to npm on staging push
requirements.background: Staging branch requires the same CI protection and testing rigor as main to catch issues before production. Expanding the Node version matrix ensures compatibility across all supported versions. Automated staging releases with @staging dist-tag enable pre-release testing without affecting the stable npm latest tag.

requirements.text: CHANGELOG.md has a `[0.2.0]` entry covering all v0.2 changes: circuit breaker (Phases 6–8), GUARD 5 gap closure (Phase 5), QGSD rebranding, quorum scoring, quorum commands, debug flow, checkpoint:verify (quick tasks 1–12)
CHANGELOG.md `[Unreleased]` section is cleared after the `[0.2.0]` entry is finalized — clean slate for v0.3+
User can define a `quorum.active` array in `qgsd.json` listing which slots participate in quorum
Quorum orchestrator reads `quorum.active` from config instead of a hardcoded agent list; only active slots are called
`check-provider-health.chs` and scoreboard tooling derive agent list from `quorum.active` rather than hardcoded arrays
Default `quorum.active` is auto-populated at install/migration time based on discovered slots in `~/.claude.json`
Global config at `~/.claude/qgsd.json` — installed once, applies to all projects
Per-project override at `.claude/qgsd.json` — merged with global, project values take precedence
Config contains: `quorum_commands` (array of command names), `required_models` (dict of MCP tool entries: { tool_prefix, required }), `fail_mode` (open|closed, default: open)
Fail-open behavior: when a quorum model is unavailable, Stop hook passes and logs reduced quorum notification
Config validates on read — malformed config falls back to hardcoded defaults with warning
qgsd.json schema extended with `circuit_breaker.oscillation_depth` (integer, default: 3) — minimum commits touching same file set to trigger breaker
qgsd.json schema extended with `circuit_breaker.commit_window` (integer, default: 6) — number of recent commits to analyze
Circuit breaker config values validated on load; invalid values fall back to defaults with stderr warning
Two-layer config merge (global + project) applies to `circuit_breaker` settings identically to existing merge behavior
User can rotate API keys across multiple slots in a single batch flow from the main menu
Key validity status persists to `qgsd.json` after each health probe (enables DISP-03 badge to survive across sessions without re-probing)
User can open a live health dashboard from main menu showing all slots' provider, model, and health status
Dashboard refreshes on keypress (space / r) with a visible "last updated" timestamp shown at bottom
Dashboard exits cleanly on Q or Escape, returning to main menu with stdin fully restored (no character-swallowing)
`.formal/debt.json` stores debt entries with schema: id, fingerprint, title, occurrences, first_seen, last_seen, environments[], status, formal_ref, source_entries[]
Debt entries are deduplicated by fingerprint — new observations with matching fingerprints increment occurrence count and update last_seen instead of creating duplicates
Debt status follows a state machine: `open` → `acknowledged` → `resolving` → `resolved`, with only forward transitions allowed (no reopening resolved debt)
A retention policy limits debt.json growth: entries older than a configurable max_age (default 90 days) with status `resolved` are archived to `.formal/debt-archive.jsonl`
Each debt entry can link to a formal reference (model file, parameter key, requirement ID) via the `formal_ref` field
`/qgsd:observe` writes new or updated debt entries after each run; the human triage step sets status to `acknowledged` for entries the user selects to work on
`bin/analyze-state-space.cjs` reads TLA+ .cfg files and model variables to estimate state-space size per model and classifies risk as MINIMAL/LOW/MODERATE/HIGH based on configurable thresholds
The state-space analyzer flags models using unbounded domains (Nat, Int without constraints) as HIGH risk
When a model is split into sub-models, the traceability matrix validates that no requirement loses coverage (pre-split coverage >= post-split coverage for every affected requirement)
The state-space analysis report is included in the traceability matrix output as a `state_space` section per model

`analyze-state-space.cjs` identifies model pairs sharing source files or requirement prefixes, estimates the merged state space, and recommends merge when combined TLC runtime < 5 minutes — or flags the interface contract needed when merge would exceed the time budget
PreToolUse hook intercepts Bash tool calls and checks whether the current context has an active circuit breaker before running detection
Hook retrieves last N commits' changed files via `git log --name-only` (N = commit_window config) when detection is needed

Hook identifies oscillation when the exact same file set (strict set equality, not intersection) appears in ≥ oscillation_depth of the last commit_window commits — strict equality prevents false positives during TDD cycles where different files are touched per commit
Read-only Bash commands (git log, git diff, grep, cat, ls, head, tail, find) pass through without detection or blocking
Detection is skipped (returns pass) when no git repository exists in the current working directory
Conformance trace divergence rate reduced to <5% (ACHIEVED: 0% on mapped events; VERIFICATION.md missing)
`attribute-trace-divergence.cjs` walks XState guards to root-cause divergences (IMPLEMENTED; VERIFICATION.md missing)
Pivot decision surfaced in `.formal/diff-report.md` with complete attribution (DELIVERED; VERIFICATION.md missing)
Support --focus="<phrase>" flag in nf:solve to filter diagnostics and reporting output to requirements matching the focus topic via tokenization and weighted multi-field scoring

Provide /nf:mcp-repair skill to auto-diagnose quorum slot connectivity issues and apply automatic repairs where possible (restart downed MCP servers, report non-fixable issues with actionable guidance).
requirements.background: Users currently must manually diagnose and fix individual quorum slot failures (MCP servers down, CLI auth expired, quota exhausted). This skill automates the diagnosis-repair-verify cycle, improving user experience and reliability when quorum infrastructure encounters transient or fixable issues.

requirements.text: Proximity graph orchestration skills — /nf:proximity runs the 5-step proximity pipeline (graph build, candidate discovery, Haiku evaluation, semantic scoring, pairing generation) with progress reporting and summary dashboard; /nf:resolve auto-detects and triages proximity pairings alongside solve items with confirm/reject/skip actions and batch-confirmation mode (--auto-confirm-yes/--auto-reject-no).
requirements.background: The v0.34 proximity graph pipeline scripts existed in bin/ but lacked a user-facing skill to invoke them. This requirement unifies proximity graph analysis with the existing solve item triage workflow by providing two complementary skills.

requirements.text: Proximity skill Step 4 attempts semantic evaluation via haiku-semantic-eval.cjs script first, then falls back to inline Haiku sub-agent evaluation on script failure, producing identical verdict/confidence/reasoning output schema in both paths.
requirements.background: Inside Claude Code, the subprocess environment lacks ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, causing haiku-semantic-eval.cjs to fail. The sub-agent fallback uses Task(model='haiku') which works via the user's Claude subscription, ensuring /nf:proximity completes evaluation steps even when direct API access is unavailable.

requirements.text: Proximity pipeline pre-filters false positive candidates using category-domain gating, coverage checks, keyword overlap, and type-aware scoring penalties before Haiku evaluation.
requirements.background: The proximity graph produces high false positive rates because weak paths accumulate into inflated scores. This reduces FPs at multiple layers before expensive Haiku evaluation.

requirements.text: Non-neighbor pair discovery surfaces zero-path (model, requirement) pairs ranked by coverage-gap heuristic, identifying under-covered blind spots via top-N selection with configurable limit.
requirements.background: The proximity pipeline previously discovered only graph-reachable pairs via BFS. Coverage-gap ranking exposes candidates ranked by how under-covered each model and requirement are, surfacing diagnostically relevant blind spots.

requirements.text: Statusline hook detects context window size (200K, 1M, or unknown) from explicit context_window_size property or model display_name and scales token-usage color thresholds proportionally to detected context tier
requirements.background: The statusline previously assumed a hardcoded 1M context size, causing 200K sessions to display incorrect token estimates and misleading color indicators. This requirement implements context tier detection via a cascading fallback (explicit property > display_name parsing > unknown) and proportional threshold scaling so that 200K sessions show appropriate green/yellow/orange/red boundaries (20K/40K/70K) while 1M sessions preserve exact historical behavior (100K/200K/350K).

requirements.text: qgsd-prompt.js runs a fast health probe (<3s) per provider before building the dispatch list -- dead providers' slots excluded from DISPATCH_LIST
qgsd-prompt.js reads scoreboard `availability` windows and excludes slots whose `available_at` is in the future from dispatch
Dispatch list ordered by recent success rate (from scoreboard slot stats) rather than static FALLBACK-01 tier sequence -- most reliable slots dispatched first

Prompt construction (Mode A/B, Round 1/2+, artifact injection, prior_positions, review_context, improvements request) happens deterministically in JavaScript (call-quorum-slot.cjs or wrapper), not via LLM string manipulation in the slot worker agent

Output parsing (verdict, reasoning, citations, improvements extraction from raw CLI output) happens in JavaScript with structured YAML output -- the slot worker agent returns script output verbatim without LLM post-processing

Quorum preflight implements two-layer parallel health probe via --probe flag: Layer 1 spawns CLI binaries with health_check_args (3s timeout), Layer 2 hits GET /models on ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL for ccr slots (5s timeout, TTL-cached with URL normalization), filtering dead slots before dispatch.
requirements.background: Pre-dispatch slot filtering reduces wasted quorum invocations on unreachable providers. Layer 1 detects missing CLI binaries; Layer 2 validates upstream API connectivity. URL normalization and TTL caching prevent redundant probes across multiple preflight runs.

requirements.text: Quorum prompts MUST be delivered to slot CLIs without shell interpretation, character escaping, or content modification. The dispatched prompt must be byte-identical to the composed prompt.
requirements.background: Fix for SHELL-ESCAPE-01 bug where ccr slots using shell:true internally caused backticks and $ in prompts to be interpreted by /bin/sh. Fixed by piping prompts via stdin for ccr-based slots.

requirements.text: Deduplicate quorum slots that share the same underlying model; retain the first slot for each unique model in primary dispatch and demote duplicate-model slots to a MODEL-DEDUP fallback tier positioned between T1 and T2 to maximize LLM diversity within the fan-out budget.
requirements.background: The quorum initially dispatched all available slots without considering model redundancy. When codex-1 and codex-2 both ran gpt-5.4, or gemini-1 and gemini-2 both ran gemini-3-pro, the quorum wasted a primary dispatch slot on a duplicate perspective. Model deduplication ensures diverse model viewpoints by demoting duplicate-model slots to fallback tier.

requirements.text: `check-trace-schema-drift.cjs` detects schema drift and emits `check-results.ndjson`
Schema drift check runs as CI step in `run-formal-verify.cjs`
When circuit breaker is active, hook returns `hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision:'deny'` blocking Bash execution
Block reason names the oscillating file set, confirms circuit breaker is active, and lists allowed operations (read-only Bash)

Block reason instructs Claude to perform root cause analysis and map dependencies before resuming; explicitly instructs the user to manually commit the fix (since Claude cannot run git commit while blocked)
Instrumentation map (`instrumentation-map.json`) catalogs every hook/handler emission point and maps to state variables with coverage percentages
Trace corpus management indexes conformance events by session, action type, and state transition with queryable metadata
Failure taxonomy classifies all check-result failures into 5 categories (crash, timeout, logic-violation, drift, degradation)
State candidate extractor mines traces to suggest state variables and transitions that hand-written models may have missed
Canonical event vocabulary defines a strict vocabulary of event names that all layers use, preventing schema drift between layers
Quorum slot workers performing review-only tasks (verification, code review) run with restricted tool access (Read/Grep/Glob only), reducing blast radius of misbehaving agents
call-quorum-slot.cjs retries a failed slot call up to 2 times with exponential backoff (1s, 3s) before recording UNAVAIL in quorum-failures.json
providers.json contains explicit slot-to-provider mapping; when a provider probe returns DOWN, all slots on that provider skipped in a single dispatch decision
Issues are fingerprinted using a hierarchical strategy: exception type → function name → message pattern hash, producing a stable fingerprint string
Drifts are fingerprinted by formal parameter key (e.g., `MCsafety.cfg:MaxDeliberation`) — identical parameter keys always map to the same fingerprint
Cross-source deduplication uses fingerprint matching first, then Levenshtein similarity (configurable threshold, default 0.85) on titles for near-duplicates from different sources
When two debt entries are merged by fingerprint, source_entries[] from both are preserved and the entry with higher occurrence count is kept as primary
Gate A (Grounding) measures alignment as `grounding_score = traces_explained / total_traces` with target >= 80%, classifying unexplained traces
Gate B (Abstraction) enforces every L3 artifact has `derived_from` links to L2 sources with no orphaned hazards
Gate C (Validation) ensures every L3 finding maps to a concrete executable test scenario
Progressive gate maturity tracks per-model gate enforcement level (ADVISORY -> SOFT_GATE -> HARD_GATE)

Context payload size guardrail — All data passed through LLM agent context (Bash output, inter-agent JSON, skill output contracts) MUST stay under 128KB. Scripts producing large outputs MUST write to disk and pass only summaries or file-path references through the agent chain. Violations cause silent freezes with no error signal.
requirements.background: Discovered when git-heatmap.cjs --json produced 2.9MB of output that froze the solve-diagnose agent.

requirements.text: User Guide updated with TUI screenshots cross-referenced to feature descriptions
User Guide "Getting Started" walkthrough improved with step-by-step flow and screenshots
After each deliberation round, system computes P(consensus | remaining rounds); if P < threshold (default 10%), escalation fires early instead of exhausting maxDeliberation
When verify-quorum-health detects P(consensus) < 95%, it recommends and auto-adjusts maxDeliberation in qgsd.json (with user approval)
`validate health` produces zero W005 false positives for versioned phase directories (`v0.X-YY-name` format)
`validate health` produces zero W007 false positives for versioned phase directories
`validate health` produces zero W002 false positives for versioned phase references in STATE.md
R3.6 quorum enforcement reviewed and improvement areas identified
Quorum slot dispatch updated to parallel sibling Task calls (R3.2 requirement)
Slot worker protocol hardened against missing/malformed responses
Improvement validated with quorum test runs
Installer detects no configured quorum agents and prompts user to run `/qgsd:mcp-setup`
QGSD's package.json pins GSD version — version lockstep ensures hook compatibility
Installer writes hooks to `~/.claude/settings.json` directly (not plugin.json hooks — stdout is silently discarded per GitHub #10225)
Installer adds UserPromptSubmit and Stop hook entries to `~/.claude/settings.json` hooks section
Installer performs validation before registering hooks: checks MCPs are configured in Claude Code settings, warns if Codex/Gemini/OpenCode not found
Installer is idempotent — running `npx qgsd@latest` again updates hooks and config without duplicating entries
Installer respects existing per-project `.claude/qgsd.json` overrides during updates
Installer registers PreToolUse circuit breaker hook in `~/.claude/settings.json` alongside existing hooks
Installer writes default `circuit_breaker` config block to qgsd.json on first install
Reinstall (idempotent) adds missing `circuit_breaker` config block without overwriting user-modified values

Quick workflow auto-detects protected branches and creates feature branches on protected branches, commits directly on feature branches, with configurable branch templates and --no-branch escape hatch.
requirements.background: Quick tasks running on protected branches risk accidental commits that require reverting. This ensures the quick workflow safely handles branch creation with config-driven protected branch lists and glob pattern support.

requirements.text: Layer manifest (`layer-manifest.json`) registers every formal artifact with its layer, gate relationships, and grounding status
`nf-solve` gains three new layer-transition sweeps (L1->L2, L2->L3, L3->TC) in its convergence loop
`run-formal-verify` gains three new gate check step groups that produce alignment scores and gap reports
Cross-layer alignment dashboard shows L1 coverage %, Gate A score, Gate B score, Gate C score in single terminal view
Model registry (`model-registry.json`) extended with `source_layer`, `gate_maturity`, and `layer_maturity` fields per model
All existing 43+ formal models classified into layer architecture with maturity level (Level 0-3)
User can set or update the API key for any agent through the wizard
Key is stored securely via keytar (bin/secrets.cjs)
Wizard writes key from keytar to `~/.claude.json` mcpServers env block during apply
Wizard automatically restarts the agent after key changes take effect
CI step detects liveness properties lacking a fairness declaration in `invariants.md` and emits `result=inconclusive` instead of `pass`
`run-formal-verify.cjs` STEPS includes a `ci:liveness-fairness-lint` step that enforces LIVE-01
PRISM always uses current scoreboard rates via `export-prism-constants` pre-step calibration
PostToolUse hook `qgsd-spec-regen.js` auto-regenerates TLA+/Alloy specs on XState machine changes
`sensitivity-sweep-feedback.cjs` detects threshold violations and triggers PRISM re-run
`propose-debug-invariants.cjs` mines TLA+ invariant candidates from debug sessions
Auto error extraction at session end — install.js must register nf-session-end.js as SessionEnd hook so re-install does not silently drop it
User correction capture — SessionEnd hook persists user corrections to memory store on session close
Quorum-validated skill extraction — skill-extractor.cjs is invoked automatically by nf-session-end.js (not orphaned)
Failure catalog with confidence — failure entries persist across sessions via SessionEnd hook pipeline
User can set per-slot latency budgets in providers.json; slots exceeding their budget are cut off mid-dispatch instead of blocking the quorum pipeline indefinitely
Installer reads `~/.claude.json` to auto-detect MCP server names for Codex, Gemini, OpenCode
Detection matches server names containing "codex", "gemini", "opencode" (case-insensitive keyword match)
Detected names written to `~/.claude/qgsd.json` as `required_models` on install
If detection finds no matching servers, installer falls back to hardcoded defaults: `mcp__codex-cli__`, `mcp__gemini-cli__`, `mcp__opencode__`
User can manually edit `qgsd.json` to override detected names
Stop hook matches tool_use names by prefix (e.g. `mcp__codex-cli__` matches both `mcp__codex-cli__codex` and `mcp__codex-cli__review`)
`.formal/spec/mcp-calls/` contains TLA+ model of MCP environment behavior
`MCMCPEnv` added to `SURFACE_MAP` and `VALID_CONFIGS`; `invariants.md` created with EventualDecision fairness declarations; CI step added to `formal-verify.yml`
MCP availability rates from `run-prism.cjs` feed into the MCP environment model
`readMCPAvailabilityRates` exported with composite-key filter; `module.exports` always reachable via require.main guard; prism:mcp-availability STEPS entry and CI step added
Decisions persist across compaction — memory-store.cjs JSONL append for decisions category injected by PreCompact hook
Proactive session reminders — SessionStart hook generates memory reminder from recent entries across all categories
Error resolution memory — error patterns persist to JSONL with queryable interface
Quorum decision memory — quorum verdicts persist to JSONL for cross-session learning
GSD planning commands within this repo (new-project, plan-phase, etc.) auto-resolve questions via quorum before escalating to user
Only questions where quorum fails to reach consensus are presented to the user
Auto-resolved questions are presented as a list of assumptions before escalated questions
User can have multiple `claude-*` slots (`claude-1` through `claude-N`) each running a different model or provider
User can have multiple `copilot-N`, `opencode-N`, `codex-cli-N`, and `gemini-cli-N` slots as separate `~/.claude.json` entries
Adding a new slot of any family is supported by both direct config edit and via the mcp-setup wizard
Installer auto-syncs all provider slots from `providers.json` to `~/.claude.json` MCP entries and `quorum_active`, creating missing slots without modifying existing entries
requirements.background: Provider slots (codex-2, gemini-2) are defined in providers.json with full configuration but lack corresponding ~/.claude.json MCP entries, preventing quorum dispatch to these subscription slots. Auto-syncing ensures every provider slot has a working MCP entry and is discoverable by the quorum orchestrator without manual user configuration.

requirements.text: Key operations produce structured log output on stderr
Source types are pluggable — adding a new source type requires only a new fetch handler function and config entry, not changes to the observe orchestrator
`/qgsd:observe` supports `prometheus` source type that executes a PromQL query against a configured endpoint and maps results to the standard issue schema
`/qgsd:observe` supports `grafana` source type that fetches active alerts from a configured Grafana instance
`/qgsd:observe` supports `logstash` source type that runs an Elasticsearch query against a configured endpoint and maps hits to the standard issue schema
`/qgsd:observe` presents two output tables: **Issues** (discrete events — errors, bugs, feedback) and **Drifts** (quantitative divergences — formal parameter vs production measurement)
Observe config lives in `.planning/observe-sources.md` YAML frontmatter (replacing `triage-sources.md`), backward-compatible with existing triage config format
Each source fetch handler has a configurable timeout and fail-open behavior — one failing source does not block the others
CLI tools exit with appropriate codes (0 = success, non-zero = failure) and write errors to stderr, output to stdout
Observe handler integrates Category 16 that reads quality-filtered accumulated error patterns from errors.jsonl and routes them as internal issues to the solve debt pipeline
requirements.background: The errors.jsonl memory store accumulates error patterns automatically during transcript learning, but had no consumption path into the solve system. This requirement closes that feedback loop by surfacing validated error entries as observable issues, enabling automated debt tracking and resolution. Quality filtering at extraction time removes file dumps, JSON blobs, and noise.

requirements.text: Agent payload size monitoring — All scripts invoked by LLM-facing skills that produce machine-readable output (--json mode) MUST be auditable for output size. A pre-flight check or CI validation SHOULD flag any script whose stdout output exceeds 128KB when invoked from an agent context. Complements GUARD-01 (context payload size guardrail) with automated detection.
requirements.background: Without monitoring, GUARD-01 violations are only discovered when an agent freezes. This requirement ensures violations are caught proactively.

requirements.text: Domain-specific context retrieval — context-retriever.cjs analyzes task domain and fetches relevant context artifacts
Phase-to-phase context accumulation — context-stack.cjs maintains JSONL phase index for cross-phase context injection
Specialized retrieval agents — quorum dispatch enriches prompts with domain-relevant context via enrichPromptWithRetrieval
When oscillation is detected and the oscillating file set contains only internal code files, Claude MUST enter oscillation resolution mode instead of hard-stopping
Oscillation resolution mode presents oscillation evidence (file set, commit graph) to all available quorum models with structural-coupling framing
Quorum deliberates (R3.3, up to 4 rounds) and may only approve unified solutions — partial/incremental fixes are rejected
On consensus, Claude presents the unified solution plan to the user for approval before any execution
If no consensus after 4 rounds, Claude hard-stops and escalates to the human with all model positions
Worktree-isolated executor subagents — nf-worktree-executor agent runs plans in isolated git worktrees
Parallel plan execution with merge orchestration — Pattern D in execute-plan.md dispatches independent plans to worktrees with merge queue coordination
`bin/qgsd-solve.cjs` includes a P->F (Production -> Formal) residual layer that reads `.formal/debt.json` and compares acknowledged drift entries against formal model thresholds
The P->F residual count equals the number of acknowledged debt entries where production measurements diverge from formal parameter values
Solve operates only on debt entries with status `acknowledged` — `open` entries are ignored until a human triages them
During a solve cycle, observations are frozen — new observe runs do not modify debt entries that are in `resolving` status
P->F remediation dispatches `/qgsd:quick` tasks to update formal model parameters when production reality has legitimately changed, or flags investigation when production has regressed
`generate-proposed-changes.cjs` auto-synthesizes TLA+ deltas from PLAN.md truths
`run-phase-tlc.cjs` provides iterative TLC verification loop gating planning (3-attempt cap)
`quorum-formal-context.cjs` generates formal evidence blocks for quorum slots
User can set quorum timeout (ms) per slot from a dedicated menu shortcut — not buried inside editAgent
User can configure update policy per slot: auto / prompt / skip
Auto-update policy check runs on manage-agents startup for slots configured as `auto`
User can export full roster config to a portable JSON file — all API key values replaced with `__redacted__` placeholders
User can import roster config from JSON file — validates schema, prompts to re-enter any redacted key, confirms before applying
Import creates a timestamped backup of `~/.claude.json` before applying any changes
User can configure hook execution priority in nf.json so safety hooks (circuit-breaker) always run before enhancement hooks (prompt injection), with deterministic ordering guaranteed
User can change the base URL (provider) for an existing agent
Wizard offers curated provider list (AkashML, Together.xyz, Fireworks) + custom entry
Wizard updates `~/.claude.json` ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL and restarts agent on apply
User can select a named provider preset (AkashML / Together.xyz / Fireworks.ai) in addAgent/editAgent flow instead of manually typing a URL
User can clone an existing slot — copies provider URL and model config, prompts for new slot name
plan-milestone-gaps proposed gap closure phases are submitted to R3 quorum for approval before ROADMAP.md is updated (replaces AskUserQuestion confirmation gate)
execute-phase gaps_found triggers quorum diagnosis and auto-resolution (replaces chain halt + manual suggestion)
discuss-phase remaining user_questions (surviving R4 pre-filter) are routed to quorum in auto mode (replaces AskUserQuestion for gray areas)
TUI section promoted from `<details>` to visible section with hero screenshot above the fold
"Who This Is For" expanded to 3-4 bullet points framing concrete problems nForma solves (single-model blind spots, context rot, oscillation loops, manual tracking)
"With vs. Without" comparison table added after "Who This Is For" showing Claude Code alone vs. with nForma
"By the Numbers" quantified metrics section added near top (agents, commands, requirements, hooks, formal models, milestones)
Architecture/quorum flow diagram added to "How It Works" intro showing prompt → dispatch → consensus → execute flow
"What's New in v0.32" changelog section added near top of README
Nav bar expanded with additional anchor links (TUI, Configuration, Star History)
Community/Contributing section added before Star History with Discord CTA and contribution guidelines
Getting Started collapsible sections rebalanced — critical path (install → quorum setup → first command) visible by default, advanced options collapsed
Broken table in Observability section fixed (solve screenshot moved outside table rows)
`npx qgsd --reset-breaker` CLI flag clears `.claude/circuit-breaker-state.json` and logs confirmation — enables manual recovery when circuit breaker deadlocks due to blocked git commit
`check-trace-redaction.cjs` checks traces for PII/secret patterns and emits `check-results.ndjson`
Redaction check runs as CI step in `run-formal-verify.cjs`
Redaction violations emit `result=fail` (not warn)
Graceful degradation on dependency failure
Structured error handling with fail-open pattern
Failures in external services (APIs, databases, third-party SDKs) are caught and handled gracefully — the application degrades functionality rather than crashing
Long-running operations (file uploads, data processing, API calls >2s) show progress indication and can be cancelled by the user
`git mv get-shit-done/ qgsd-core/` — directory renamed, git history preserved
`bin/install.js` updated to read from `qgsd-core/` instead of `get-shit-done/` — `node bin/install.js --claude --global` succeeds
All hardcoded `get-shit-done/` path strings removed from `bin/gsd-tools.cjs`, workflow files, agent files, and template files
`~/.claude/qgsd/` installed runtime is functionally identical before and after rename (verified by spot-check)
`package.json` version field bumped from `0.1.0` to `0.2.0`
v0.2 milestone archived in `MILESTONES.md` — includes What Shipped, Phases (6–10), Requirements Satisfied (all 20 v0.2 requirements), Key Decisions Carried Forward
Git tag `v0.2.0` created at release commit and pushed to remote
`qgsd@0.2.0` published to npm registry — `npx qgsd@0.2.0` installs successfully
Hazard model applies FMEA (Severity x Occurrence x Detection = RPN) to L2 operational model states and transitions
Failure mode catalog enumerates concrete failure modes per L2 state/transition with effects and severity classification
Risk heatmap produces ranked transition list (highest-risk-first) combining FMEA severity with coverage gap data
Model-driven test generation produces test recipe JSON from L3 failure modes with input sequence, expected outcome, and oracle
Design impact analysis traces a git diff through all three layers showing instrumentation, state, and hazard effects
`--repair` cannot silently overwrite a rich STATE.md (>50 lines) without explicit `--force` flag; guard fires and repair is skipped with a message
Legacy pre-versioning numeric phase dirs 18-39 are archived to `.planning/archive/legacy/`
Formal verification in target repos uses only the target repo's own models; nForma-internal model generators and checkers are excluded from cross-repo execution
requirements.background: When /nf:solve runs in target repos, nForma-internal formal models should not be generated into or checked against external repos. Only the target repo's own models (discovered dynamically from .planning/formal/) are verified.

requirements.text: Scoreboard tracks performance by slot name (`claude-1`, `copilot-1`) — slot is the stable key
Each scoreboard entry displays the current model loaded in that slot as context
When a slot's model changes, a new scoreboard row is created for that slot

`model-registry.json` entries gain a `requirements` array (string[]) listing the requirement IDs each model covers; existing entries seeded from the property-to-requirement map in TRACEABILITY_RESEARCH.md
`check-result.schema.json` gains a `requirement_ids` array (string[]) field; validation updated to accept the new field
Each verification runner (run-tlc.cjs, run-alloy.cjs, run-prism.cjs, CI lint steps) emits `requirement_ids` in its NDJSON output, extracted from model-registry or inline annotations
`requirements.json` envelope gains an optional `formal_models` array (string[]) per requirement, listing model file paths that verify it
Pre-commit hook runs secret scanning to block commits containing credentials
CI pipeline runs deep secret scanning across full repo history on every PR
All external input is validated and sanitized at system boundaries before processing
Dependencies are scanned for known vulnerabilities in CI
Pre-commit hook runs secret scanning (e.g., Gitleaks) to block commits containing API keys, tokens, passwords, or credentials
CI pipeline runs deep secret scanning (e.g., TruffleHog) across full repo history on every PR
All external input (user input, API request bodies, query parameters, file uploads) is validated and sanitized at system boundaries before processing
Dependencies are scanned for known vulnerabilities in CI (e.g., npm audit, Dependabot, Snyk) and critical/high findings block merge
Invariant catalog aggregates declared invariants from specs with observed invariants mined from traces into a unified catalog
Mismatch register tracks every L2-model vs L1-trace disagreement as a JSONL ledger with resolution status
Assumption register catalogs assumptions made when deriving L2 from L1 with validation status and linked L2 states
Operational state machine derivation produces an observed-behavior FSM from traces comparable to hand-written TLA+/XState models

`bin/run-sensitivity-sweep.cjs` varies key model parameters (at minimum: quorum size N across its full range, timeout threshold T across low/medium/high values) across ≥3 values each, running TLA+/PRISM checks for each configuration, and records outcome deltas (pass→fail, pass→inconclusive transitions) in `.formal/sensitivity-report.ndjson` using v2.1 schema — each record annotated with which parameter value caused the transition.

`plan-phase.md` step 8.3 (FV gate) is extended to also run `run-sensitivity-sweep.cjs` (fail-open) and inject `SENSITIVITY_CONTEXT` into the quorum `review_context`, surfacing the top-3 highest-impact parameters as planning recommendations: which code paths control them, which boundary values to test, and which metrics to monitor.

`bin/sensitivity-report.cjs` generates `.formal/sensitivity-report.md` — a human-readable ranked list of sensitive parameters by outcome-flip count, each annotated with (a) the code path or configuration variable that controls it, (b) recommended unit/integration test cases for boundary values, (c) recommended monitoring metrics to track in production.
`.claude/rules/` files are tagged with relevance patterns (tool names, file globs, keywords) so only matching rules load per turn, measurably reducing per-turn token consumption
`detect-coverage-gaps.cjs` diffs TLC states vs conformance traces to produce `.formal/coverage-gaps.md`
`generate-petri-net.cjs --roadmap` models phase dependencies as Petri net with critical path analysis
`prism-priority.cjs` ranks roadmap items by PRISM failure probability
`quorum-consensus-gate.cjs` gates quorum rounds by Poisson binomial consensus probability threshold
User sees all quorum agents referred to by slot name (`claude-1`, `copilot-1`, `gemini-cli-1`, etc.) in all QGSD output and commands
Migration script renames existing `~/.claude.json` mcpServers entries from model-based names to slot names automatically (non-destructive, invertible)
All QGSD source files (hooks, orchestrator, commands, scoreboard tooling) updated to use new slot names — no old names remain
`mcp-status`, `mcp-set-model`, `mcp-update`, `mcp-restart` accept and display slot names correctly
The unified MCP server SHALL expose all configured providers as MCP tools over stdio JSON-RPC, driven by providers.json
The circuit breaker hook SHALL detect commit oscillation patterns and trigger automatic resolution (injecting oscillation-resolution-mode context) instead of blocking tool use
The prompt hook SHALL inject quorum dispatch instructions into user prompts and provide automatic recovery context when the circuit breaker is active
The stop hook SHALL block planning responses that lack quorum verification, enforcing R3.1/R3.2 compliance
The nForma TUI SHALL provide a terminal dashboard for project status, sessions, agents, and configuration management
The solve engine SHALL compute residual vectors across all traceability layers and report health, format, and JSON output

The solve engine SHALL display triage breakdown (FP count, archived count, actionable count) alongside residual vectors in report output, and re-surface stale archived items when underlying files have been modified
requirements.background: Users encountered confusion when solve reports showed large residual counts but /nf:resolve found nothing to act on. Enriching report output with triage disposition and implementing staleness checks (>30 days with modified files) maintains data freshness.

requirements.text: The solve engine SHALL compute and persist net_residual counts for human-gated layers (d_to_c, c_to_r, t_to_r, d_to_r) by subtracting Haiku false-positive classifications and archived items from raw sweep residuals
requirements.background: Raw residual counts in solve-state.json overstate actionable work because they include items already classified as false positives by Haiku or archived through /nf:resolve. Downstream consumers (TUI, skill-overview) require accurate residual counts to report correct progress. Net residual provides the true actionable item count for each layer.

requirements.text: Stop hook formalized in `QGSDStopHook.tla` + `MCStopHook.cfg`, TLC verifies safety + liveness
`QGSDOscillation.tla` audited against `qgsd-circuit-breaker.js` — no drift detected
Quorum composition verified in `quorum-composition.als` — 3 composition rules hold
`generate-phase-spec.cjs` translates PLAN.md `must_haves: truths:` to TLA+ PROPERTY stubs

Multi-adapter FSM-to-TLA+ transpiler supports 10 state machine frameworks (XState v5/v4, javascript-state-machine, Robot, AWS Step Functions, Stately, Python transitions, sismic, looplab/fsm, qmuntal/stateless) through a plugin architecture with shared MachineIR intermediate representation.
requirements.background: Quick task 292 generalized the monolithic xstate-to-tla.cjs into a plugin-based transpiler to enable formal verification of state machines from any framework, not just XState.

requirements.text: audit-milestone updates STATE.md "Stopped at" and "Current Position" fields with the audit result (passed / gaps_found / tech_debt) after writing the MILESTONE-AUDIT.md artifact
State schema: `{ active, file_set[], activated_at, commit_window_snapshot[] }` — captures what triggered the breaker
Hook reads existing state first — if active, applies enforcement immediately without re-running git log detection
State file created silently if absent; failure to write logs to stderr but never blocks execution

All 4 Gen1 MCP server repos (claude, codex, copilot, openhands) ported to Gen2 per-tool architecture (`*.tool.ts` + `registry.ts`) with Gen1 files (`definitions.ts`, `handlers.ts`) removed — all repos on main branch
gemini-mcp-server npm package name is unscoped (`gemini-mcp-server`, not `@tuannvm/gemini-mcp-server`) — `~/.claude.json` mcpServers["gemini-cli"].args reflects the unscoped name
Stop hook reads transcript JSONL for tool_use entries matching configured quorum model names
Stop hook checks `stop_hook_active` flag first — if true, exits 0 immediately (infinite loop prevention)
Stop hook checks `hook_event_name` — if `SubagentStop`, exits 0 immediately (subagent exclusion)
Stop hook scopes transcript search to current turn only (lines since last user message boundary) — survives context compaction

Stop hook reads transcript JSONL as the authoritative source of quorum evidence — no fast-path pre-check (design decision: last_assistant_message substring matching is not a reliable signal; JSONL parse is synchronous and correct for all transcript sizes)
Stop hook verifies quorum only when a configured planning command was issued in the current turn (scope filtering)
Stop hook blocks with `{"decision": "block", "reason": "..."}` when quorum is missing — reason includes exact tool names and instructions

Block reason message format: "QUORUM REQUIRED: Before completing this /qgsd:[command] response, call [tool1], [tool2], [tool3] with your current plan. Present their responses, then deliver your final output."
Stop hook passes (exits 0, no decision field) when quorum evidence found or no planning command in scope
QGSD ships as separate npm package (`qgsd` or `get-shit-done-quorum`) that wraps GSD
When GSD releases a new planning command, QGSD releases a patch update adding the command to the default `quorum_commands` list
QGSD changelog explicitly tracks which GSD version it is compatible with
No QGSD code modifies any GSD source files — all additions are in separate files (`hooks/qgsd-stop.js`, `hooks/qgsd-prompt.js`, `bin/qgsd-install.js`)
Auto-compaction at clean boundaries — PreCompact hook preserves execution state and memory across compaction events
Thinking budget scaling per task type — task complexity classifier adjusts thinking budget directive based on plan vs research vs quick task
Token usage dashboard — `/nf:tokens` displays per-slot and per-session token consumption from telemetry JSONL
Task-complexity model routing — TIER_SLOT_MAP filters quorum slots by task complexity tier (T1/T2/T3)

`bin/generate-traceability-matrix.cjs` reads model-registry, requirements.json, and check-results.ndjson to produce `.formal/traceability-matrix.json` with property-level links between requirements and formal properties

The traceability matrix includes a `coverage_summary` section: total requirements, covered count, coverage percentage, list of uncovered requirements, list of orphan properties (properties with no requirement mapping)
The traceability matrix is generated as a step in `run-formal-verify.cjs` after all checks complete
Bidirectional validation detects asymmetric links (model claims requirement X but requirement X does not claim that model) and emits warnings
CI guard warns when formal coverage percentage drops below a configurable threshold (default 15%) compared to the previous traceability matrix

`bin/generate-triage-bundle.cjs` reads `check-results.ndjson` and writes `.formal/diff-report.md` (per-check delta from last run) and `.formal/suspects.md` (checks with `result=fail` or `triage_tags` set)
`run-formal-verify.cjs` calls `generate-triage-bundle.cjs` as the final step after all checks complete
All FV checkers append normalized JSON to `check-results.ndjson` — `{ tool, check_id, result, detail, ts }`
`run-formal-verify.cjs` generates `check-results.ndjson` as canonical output artifact
Triage bundle reads from `check-results.ndjson`, not tool stdout; CI enforcement steps run inside orchestrator before summary read (v0.19-11 timing fix)
CI step exits non-zero when any `result=fail` entry exists in `check-results.ndjson`

A UPPAAL timed automaton model (`.formal/uppaal/quorum-races.xml`) captures the concurrency structure of the quorum protocol — specifically when concurrent slot responses and timeout expirations fire relative to each other. The model uses `runtime_ms` bounds from `check-results.ndjson` as empirical timing constraints (clock guards and invariants), not hardcoded constants.

`bin/run-uppaal.cjs` executes the UPPAAL model checker (verifyta CLI) against `quorum-races.xml` and writes a check result to `check-results.ndjson` using the v2.1 schema (SCHEMA-01 prerequisite). The STEPS entry `uppaal:quorum-races` is added to `run-formal-verify.cjs`.

The model surfaces at least two critical measurement points as annotated properties: (a) the minimum inter-slot response gap that prevents a race condition, (b) the maximum timeout value for which the quorum can still reach consensus before the planning gate deadline.

The close-formal-gaps workflow generates UPPAAL timed automata specs when requirements specify real-time constraints, and selects Petri net models for concurrent workflows, with detailed formalism selection heuristics and generation templates integrated into steps 3–6.
requirements.background: The close-formal-gaps workflow previously referenced UPPAAL and Petri formalisms in its table but lacked generation templates, selection heuristics, and detailed instructions. This requirement captures the extension that enables Claude to generate formal specs in these formalisms for user project requirements, completing the closed-loop gap between formalism selection and model generation.

requirements.text: The model registry (model-registry.json) and complexity profiler (model-complexity-profile.cjs) scan and process UPPAAL (.xml) and Petri (.dot) models at feature parity with TLA+, Alloy, and PRISM, enabling requirement traceability and state-space profiling for all five formalisms.
requirements.background: UPPAAL and Petri net models exist on disk and are discoverable by run-formal-verify.cjs, but initialize-model-registry.cjs and model-complexity-profile.cjs only handled TLA+, Alloy, and PRISM. Extending both tools to cover UPPAAL and Petri formalisms integrates them into the requirement traceability lifecycle and enables complexity analysis across all formal verification formalisms.

requirements.text: UserPromptSubmit hook detects GSD planning commands via explicit allowlist regex match against prompt field
Allowlist contains exactly 6 commands: new-project, plan-phase, new-milestone, discuss-phase, verify-work, research-phase
UserPromptSubmit hook injects quorum instructions via `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` (not systemMessage — goes into Claude's context window)
Injected context names the exact MCP tools to call and instructs Claude to present model responses before delivering final output
UserPromptSubmit hook never fires on execute-phase or other non-planning commands
Every user-initiated action produces immediate feedback and completion feedback
Destructive actions require explicit confirmation or provide undo
Error messages are human-readable, explain what went wrong, and suggest recovery
Every user-initiated action produces immediate feedback (loading/disabled state) and completion feedback (result message, navigation change, or visible state update)
Destructive actions (delete, reset, remove, overwrite) require explicit confirmation or provide undo within a reasonable window
Error messages are human-readable, explain what went wrong, and suggest a next step or recovery action
Hook stdin JSON is validated against lightweight schemas with structured error reporting; malformed input produces a diagnostic message on stderr before fail-open exit
File-based execution state — execution-progress.cjs tracks plan/task completion state to disk, survives compaction
Continuous test verification — PostToolUse hook triggers boundary-detection checks during execution with budget cap
Machine-verifiable completion — done_conditions in task schema enable automated evaluateAllConditions check
`qgsd-verifier` agent runs `run-formal-verify` after implementation and includes `check-results.ndjson` digest in `VERIFICATION.md`
`VERIFICATION.md` template gains a `## Formal Verification` section summarizing pass/fail/warn counts per formalism (tla, alloy, prism, ci)
All TUI screenshots regenerated via VHS tape automation after README structural changes
VHS tape hardened for CI reliability (zsh shell, explicit PATH export, CLAUDECODE unset)
User can run `/qgsd:mcp-setup` to start the MCP configuration wizard
First run (no configured agents) presents a guided linear onboarding flow step by step
Re-run shows the current agent roster as a navigable menu
Each agent in the menu shows current model, provider, and key status (present/missing)
User confirms before changes are applied; wizard restarts affected agents after apply
`/qgsd:mcp-setup` re-run menu includes an "Edit Quorum Composition" option
Composition screen shows all discovered slots with on/off toggle for `quorum.active` inclusion
User can add a new slot for any family (claude, copilot, opencode, codex-cli, gemini-cli) from within the wizard, which writes the new entry to `~/.claude.json` and triggers restart
